data:
Search results for "llvmlite ir.IntType ir.FunctionType ir.Module basic example Chinese":

Title: 3.1. Types — llvmlite 0.19.0 documentation - Read the Docs
URL: https://llvmlite.readthedocs.io/en/v0.19.0/ir/types.html
Content: All values used in a LLVM module are explicitly typed. Example: int32 = ir.IntType(32) ir.FunctionType(int32,

Title: Example—defining a simple function - llvmlite - Read the Docs
URL: https://llvmlite.readthedocs.io/en/latest/user-guide/ir/examples.html
Content: * Example—defining a simple function. This example defines a function that adds 2 double-precision, floating-point numbers. """ This file demonstrates a trivial function "fpadd" returning the sum oftwo floating-point numbers. """ from  llvmlite  import ir # Create some useful types double = ir. FunctionType(double,(double, double))# Create an empty module... Module(name = __file__) # and declare a function named "fpadd" inside it func = ir. Function(module, fnty, name = "fpadd") # Now implement the function block = func. append_basic_block(name = "entry") builder = ir. IRBuilder(block) a, b = func. fadd(a, b, name = "res") builder. ret(result) # Print the module IR print(module). The generated LLVM intermediate representation is printed at the end:. ; ModuleID = "examples/ir_fpadd.py" target  triple  =  "unknown-unknown-unknown" target  datalayout  =  "" define  double  @"fpadd"(double  %".1",  double  %".2"){entry:  %"res"  =  fadd  double  %".1",  %".2"  ret  double  %"res"}. To learn how to compile and execute this function, see LLVM binding layer—llvmlite.binding.

Title: llvmlite/llvmlite/ir/module.py at main · numba/llvmlite · GitHub
URL: https://github.com/numba/llvmlite/blob/master/llvmlite/ir/module.py
Content: self.name = name # name is for debugging/informational. def \_fix\_metadata\_operands(self, operands):. # A literal None creates a null metadata value. # A literal string creates a metadata string value. op = self.add\_metadata(op). def \_fix\_di\_operands(self, operands):. for name, op in operands:. if isinstance(op, (list, tuple)):. def str\_ditok\_operands(self, operands):. if name == 'encoding' and isinstance(op, values.DIToken):. def add\_metadata(self, operands):. Add an unnamed metadata to the module with the given \*operands\*. (a sequence of values) or return a previous equivalent metadata. if not isinstance(operands, (list, tuple)):. raise TypeError("expected a list or tuple of metadata values, ". operands = self.\_fix\_metadata\_operands(operands). md = values.MDValue(self, operands, name=str(n)). \*operands\* (a dict of values with string keys) or return. di = values.DIValue(self, is\_distinct, kind, operands, name=str(n)). def add\_named\_metadata(self, name, element=None):. Add a named metadata node to the module, if it doesn't exist,. If \*element\* is a sequence of values. if name in self.namedmetadata:. if not isinstance(element, values.Value):. def get\_named\_metadata(self, name):. if name in self.globals:. return values.Function(self, fnty, name=name).

Title: Writing Fibonacci in LLVM with llvmlite - Ian Bertolacci
URL: https://ian-bertolacci.github.io/posts/writing_fibonacci_in_LLVM_with_llvmlite
Content: from __future__ import print_function from llvmlite import ir importllvmlite.binding as llvm from ctypes import CFUNCTYPE, c_int64""" Generate fibonacci function: f(n) = 1 if n <= 1 else f(n-1) + f(n-2) """ # Create a 64bit wide int type int_type = ir. Module(name = "m_fibonacci_example") # Create the Fibonacci function and block fn_fib = ir. Function(module, fn_int_to_int_type, name = "fn_fib") fn_fib_block = fn_fib. append_basic_block(name = "fn_fib_entry") # Create the builder for the fibonacci code block builder = ir. Constant(int_type, 2); # Create inequality comparison instruction fn_fib_n_lteq_1 = builder. ret(fn_fib_rec_res) # Print the generated LLVM asm code print(module)""" Execute generated code. get_function_address("fn_fib") # Run the function via ctypes c_fn_fib = CFUNCTYPE(c_int64, c_int64)(func_ptr)# Test our function for n in 0..50 for n in xrange(0, 50 + 1): result = c_fn_fib(n) print("c_fn_fib({0}) = {1}". # Create the Fibonacci function and block fn_fib = ir. Function(module, fn_int_to_int_type, name = "fn_fib") fn_fib_block = fn_fib. # Create inequality comparison instruction fn_fib_n_lteq_1 = builder.

Title: llvmlite/llvmlite/ir/types.py at main · numba/llvmlite - GitHub
URL: https://github.com/numba/llvmlite/blob/master/llvmlite/ir/types.py
Content: A lightweight LLVM python binding for writing JIT compilers. Convert this type object to an LLVM type. FunctionType(Type): """ The type for functions. for all
